[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]]) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]]) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]])
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]]

Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]]) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]]) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641798]] Wikidata name: Michinoomi-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Michinoomi no Mikoto | image = 道臣命 Michinoomi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg | image_size = | caption = Imaginary portrait of Michinoomi no Mikoto by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]], from ''Nihon Hyakushōden'' | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Hinoomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] (Ōtomo no Muraji) | parents = Father: {{ill|Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja|刺田比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|According to the ''Koya-ke Genealogy''<ref>''Kōkai-koku Ichinomiya Asama Jinja-shi,'' Asama Shrine, 1979, pp. 278-300. Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]]) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]]) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]])
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]]
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}}

[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).

'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]]) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]]) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]])
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]]

After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).

'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]]]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]]. He served as a vanguard in [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]], to open a route from [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]] to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]]) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]]) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ([[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]])
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641798]] Wikidata name: Michinoomi-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Michinoomi no Mikoto | image = 道臣命 Michinoomi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg | image_size = | caption = Imaginary portrait of Michinoomi no Mikoto by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}}, from ''Nihon Hyakushōden'' | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Hinoomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Ōtomo no Muraji) | parents = Father: {{ill|Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja|刺田比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|According to the ''Koya-ke Genealogy''<ref>''Kōkai-koku Ichinomiya Asama Jinja-shi,'' Asama Shrine, 1979, pp. 278-300. Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}. He served as a vanguard in {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu}}, to open a route from {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama}}) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera}}) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}})
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://okanomiya.com/ Official website of Sashitahiko Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Michi no Omi]][[ja:道臣命]][[zh:道臣命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Michinoomi no Mikoto | image = 道臣命 Michinoomi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg | image_size = | caption = Imaginary portrait of Michinoomi no Mikoto by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}}, from ''Nihon Hyakushōden'' | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Hinoomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | Rank = | allegiance = [[Emperor Jimmu]] | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} (Ōtomo no Muraji) | parents = Father: {{ill|Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja|刺田比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashitahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{efn|According to the ''Koya-ke Genealogy''<ref>''Kōkai-koku Ichinomiya Asama Jinja-shi,'' Asama Shrine, 1979, pp. 278-300. Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}. He served as a vanguard in {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu}}, to open a route from {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama}}) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera}}) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}})
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}}

Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera|1=Fujiidera}}) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}})

Toshiho Hoga's ''Collection of Ancient Clan Genealogies'' (1986) also references this content.</ref>}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Ajihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_achievements = }}
[[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto.jpg|thumb|220px|Michinoomi no Mikoto / from ''Zenkō Kojitsu'']] [[File:Michinoomi no Mikoto following a crow to the enemy's lair LACMA M.2000.105.97.jpg|thumb|Illustration of Michinoomi no Mikoto in ''Dai Nippon Meishōkan'' by {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|1=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}]]
'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}. He served as a vanguard in {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}, to open a route from {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera|1=Fujiidera}}) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}})
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}

For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
== Shrines Dedicated to Michinoomi no Mikoto == * {{ill|Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasihiko Shrine|lt=Sasadahiko Shrine|ja|刺田比古神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasadahiko Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}) * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera|1=Fujiidera}}) * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine|lt=Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|ja|住吉大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}) * Kerasaki Gongen Shrine ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}})
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} * {{ill|Jinmu boom|ja|神武景気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu boom|qq=}} * {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}

'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}. He served as a vanguard in {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}, to open a route from {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}}). With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.

Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).

'''Michinoomi no Mikoto''' (道臣命) is a legendary figure from ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. Initially named '''Hinoomi no Mikoto''' (日臣命), he was a descendant of {{ill|Amenooshihi no Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}. He served as a vanguard in {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern campaign and later as a palace guard upon Emperor Jimmu’s accession.
== Achievements ==
During [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]], Michinoomi led the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}} warriors, guided by the divine {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}, to open a route from {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|qid=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} to Uda (modern {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449912}}). For his accomplishments, Emperor Jimmu bestowed upon him the name "Michinoomi."
When {{ill|Eukashi|ja|兄猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eukashi|qq=}} (Eukashi no Kami) attempted to ensnare Emperor Jimmu in a trap called "Oshi," Michinoomi confronted him, declaring, "Enter the house you built for yourself." With sword and bow ready, he forced Eukashi into his trap, where Eukashi perished. Michinoomi then dismembered Eukashi’s body, and the site became known as ''Chi-no-Hara'' (Blood Field) in Uda.
After the defeat of the {{ill|Yasotakeru|lt=Yasotakeru|lt=Eighty Bandit Chiefs|ja|八十梟帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighty Bandit Chiefs|qq=}} at Kunimi-dake, Michinoomi was tasked with eradicating their remnants. He constructed a grand chamber in the village of Osaka and lured the bandits to a banquet. At the height of the feast, he signaled his troops with a {{ill|Kumebushi|ja|久米節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} war song, and they drew their swords to annihilate the enemy.
Earlier, Michinoomi had been appointed by Emperor Jimmu to serve as a priest for the worship of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, earning the honorary title ''Izuhime'' (厳媛). Despite being male, this title reflects an ancient tradition of women officiating rituals.
Upon Emperor Jimmu’s enthronement, Michinoomi performed ceremonial songs and rituals to purify negative energies. In recognition of his service, he was granted a residence in ''Tsukisaka'' (present-day Kashihara, Nara Prefecture).
